articles.headline: Liquid AI Releases LocalCowork Powered By LFM2-24B-A2B to Execute Privacy-First Agent Workflows Locally Via Model Context Protocol (MCP)
articles.description: Liquid AI has released LFM2-24B-A2B, a model optimized for local, low-latency tool dispatch, alongside LocalCowork, an open-source desktop agent application available in their Liquid4All GitHub Cookbook. The release provides a deployable architecture for running enterprise workflows entirely on-device, eliminating API calls and data egress for privacy-sensitive environments. articles.headline: UK arts must not be sacrificed for speculative AI gains, peers say
articles.description: Ministers urged to abandon plans to let tech firms use work of novelists, artists and writers without permissionThe UK’s creative industries must not be sacrificed in the pursuit of speculative gains in AI technology, a House of Lords committee has warned, as the government prepares to reveal the economic cost of proposals to change copyright rules.A report by peers has urged ministers to develop a licensing regime for the use of creative works in AI products and abandon proposals to let tech firms use the work of novelists, artists, writers and journalists without permission. articles.description: Microsoft is the first major company to say it will keep using Anthropic models in its products.

software_engineering.headline: Liquid AI Releases LocalCowork with LFM2-24B-A2B for On-Device AI Workflows
software_engineering.what_changed: Liquid AI released LFM2-24B-A2B, a model for local tool dispatch, and LocalCowork, an open-source desktop agent for privacy-first, on-device AI workflows.
software_engineering.why_care: This allows you to build and deploy AI agents that process sensitive data locally, eliminating external API calls and improving data privacy and security.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Explore the Liquid4All GitHub Cookbook to understand how to implement these local AI agent architectures in your projects.

software_engineering.headline: UK Peers Urge Licensing for AI Use of Creative Works, Rejecting Unrestricted Access
software_engineering.what_changed: A UK House of Lords committee recommended a licensing regime for AI use of creative works, opposing government plans for tech firms to use content without permission.
software_engineering.why_care: Potential changes to copyright law could impact the data available for training AI models you use or develop, affecting model capabilities and legal compliance.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Monitor UK intellectual property law developments, especially regarding AI and copyright, to understand future data sourcing implications for your AI projects.

software_engineering.headline: Microsoft Confirms Continued Use of Anthropic Products Despite Pentagon Blacklist
software_engineering.what_changed: Microsoft stated it will continue to offer Anthropic's AI products to its customers, despite the Pentagon blacklisting Anthropic.
software_engineering.why_care: This confirms that Anthropic's models remain available within Microsoft's ecosystem, ensuring continuity for your projects that rely on these integrations.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Assess your current or planned use of Anthropic models through Microsoft services to ensure alignment with your organization's compliance and vendor policies.

software_engineering.headline: Workers Report Privacy Breaches from Ray-Ban Meta Smart Glasses Footage
software_engineering.what_changed: Workers reported viewing footage from Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses showing people in private settings, raising concerns about user privacy.
software_engineering.why_care: This highlights critical privacy and data governance challenges in consumer tech, which are important considerations when you design or integrate systems involving user-generated content or IoT devices.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Review your current or planned projects involving data collection from user devices to ensure robust privacy safeguards and clear consent mechanisms are in place.

software_engineering.headline: Morgan Stanley Banker Declares 'Wartime' for Software Amid AI Shift
software_engineering.what_changed: Morgan Stanley's top tech banker described the current environment for software as 'wartime,' signaling a significant shift driven by AI.
software_engineering.why_care: This indicates a heightened competitive landscape and accelerated innovation in the software industry due to AI, requiring you to adapt quickly and integrate AI into your development strategies.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Evaluate how AI can enhance your current software projects and identify new opportunities for AI-driven development within your team this quarter.

data_ai.headline: Liquid AI Releases LFM2-24B-A2B Model and LocalCowork for On-Device AI Agents
data_ai.what_changed: Liquid AI released LFM2-24B-A2B, a model for local tool dispatch, and LocalCowork, an open-source desktop agent application.
data_ai.why_care: This allows you to build and deploy AI agent workflows that run entirely on-device, enhancing data privacy and reducing latency for sensitive applications.
data_ai.what_to_do: Explore the Liquid4All GitHub Cookbook this week to understand how to implement these privacy-first agent architectures.

data_ai.headline: UK Peers Urge Licensing for AI Training Data, Rejecting Copyright Exemptions
data_ai.what_changed: A UK House of Lords committee recommended a licensing regime for AI use of creative works, opposing proposals to let tech firms use content without permission.
data_ai.why_care: This impacts the future availability and cost of diverse datasets for training your AI models, especially for models trained on copyrighted material.
data_ai.what_to_do: Monitor UK policy developments on AI and copyright this month, as these decisions could influence global data sourcing strategies.

data_ai.headline: Microsoft Confirms Continued Anthropic Model Access Despite Pentagon Blacklist
data_ai.what_changed: Microsoft stated that Anthropic's products remain available to its customers, despite the Pentagon blacklisting Anthropic.
data_ai.why_care: This ensures your access to Anthropic's models through Microsoft's platforms is unaffected, maintaining continuity for your projects relying on these models.
data_ai.what_to_do: Review your current and planned use of Anthropic models via Microsoft to ensure alignment with any internal or client-specific compliance requirements.

data_ai.headline: Ray-Ban Meta Smart Glasses Raise Privacy Concerns Over Bathroom Footage
data_ai.what_changed: Workers reported viewing footage from Ray-Ban Meta smart glasses showing people using the bathroom, raising privacy concerns.
data_ai.why_care: This highlights critical ethical and privacy challenges in deploying AI-enabled wearable devices, which you must consider in your own product development.
data_ai.what_to_do: Re-evaluate the privacy implications and ethical guidelines for any AI products you are developing that involve data collection from personal devices.

data_ai.headline: Morgan Stanley Banker: AI Creates 'Wartime' for Software Industry
data_ai.what_changed: Morgan Stanley's top tech banker described the current software industry environment as 'wartime, not peacetime' due to the impact of AI.
data_ai.why_care: This signals a significant shift in investment and strategic priorities within the tech sector, directly impacting funding and demand for your AI projects and skills.
data_ai.what_to_do: Assess how your current AI projects align with aggressive, innovation-focused 'wartime' strategies to ensure their continued relevance and funding.
